hypothesis: The next v4k tied-vocab gate should prove one physical token row pack can serve both embedding lookup and streamed lm_head top-k before any more board-facing packaging. The key reconciliation is that the existing output-head RTL proof uses a 64-byte per-token int8 per-tensor compute pack, while the memory scorecard budgets a 68-byte rowwise-int8 storage pack with sidecar bytes.

tooling_constraint.artifact_generation_method: sed substitution write from checked-in artifacts because Python, git, ls/find, and apply_patch are unavailable
tooling_constraint.validation_scope: sed readback only; no Python replay, simulation, synthesis, or hardware access was run

reconciliation_checks_required: Profile A replays the current 64-byte per-tensor pack as both embedding lookup source and lm_head top-k source with one global vocab scale.
Profile B replays the 68-byte rowwise pack with sidecars as both embedding lookup source and lm_head top-k source with deterministic fixed-point top-k comparison.
Report top1 and top5 agreement against f32 logits for at least 8 deterministic samples when Python model tracing is available.
Report normalized RMSE distribution for logits and decoded embedding rows.
Hash the physical row bytes used by embedding lookup and lm_head streaming and prove they are the same token-row payload.
Separate compact logical BRAM estimates from banked RTL BRAM padding before quoting a board-facing resource number.

decision.rationale: The v4k on-chip path remains promising, but the next evidence should decide whether the board-facing tied storage keeps the existing 64-byte per-tensor compute pack or moves to the 68-byte rowwise pack. Changing RTL before this replay would risk measuring a storage layout that has not been justified by multi-sample accuracy.
decision.reject: No v4k board replay, OpenFPGALoader, JTAG, FTDI/MPSSE polling, or full TinyStories synthesis was attempted in this no-board run.
decision.next_gate: When Python and normal shell tooling recover, implement scripts/task6/check_v4k_tied_vocab_rowpack_topk.py to run both profiles, regenerate this artifact from source weights, and choose the smaller passing row-pack contract.
